title: Effectiveness of Yoga in Modulating Markers of Immunity and Inflammation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
headline: Effectiveness of Yoga in Modulating Markers of Immunity and Inflammation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysi
summary: A mechanistic/observational study of yoga examined brain & mechanisms. Chronic inflammation is central to the pathogenesis of many chronic inflammatory conditions.
keyFindings: Chronic inflammation is central to the pathogenesis of many chronic inflammatory conditions.

This review aims to analyze whether the practice of yoga, or yogic meditation and breathing, has any effect on the levels of inflammatory cytokines and other inflammatory markers in patients with various chronic inflammatory diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis, neoplastic disorders, and asthma, as well as in healthy subjects, compared to usual care or sham interventions.
limitations: Only two studies had a low risk of bias (RoB); 24 other studies had a high RoB.
Observational/mechanistic design cannot establish that the practice caused the observed differences.

The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared breath-regulation component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: Effectiveness of Yoga in Modulating Markers of Immunity and Inflammation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysi
aiSnippet: Cross-sectional / Mechanistic, Cureus (2024). Chronic inflammation is central to the pathogenesis of many chronic inflammatory conditions. This review aims to analyze whether the practice of yoga, or yogic meditation and breathing, has any effect on the levels of inflammatory cytokines and other inflammatory markers in patients with various chronic inflammatory diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis, neoplastic disorders, and asthma, as well as in healthy subjects, compared to usual care or sham interventions.
whyItMatters: MoM shares breath regulation with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
